museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: March 2007: PlayStation 3 launches in Europe on March 23.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: God of War II
items.body: Sony's old console gets one more thunderous spectacle.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies; North America March 2007, Europe later.

items.title: S.T.A.L.K.E.R.: Shadow of Chernobyl
items.body: A long-awaited PC shooter turns bleak atmosphere into a whole ecology.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2007.

items.title: Crackdown
items.body: A superhero sandbox becomes both its own pleasure and a Halo 3 beta ticket.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 2007.

items.title: BioShock
items.body: Rapture makes objectivism, audio diaries and wrench combat mainstream conversation.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for August 2007.

items.title: Halo 3
items.body: Master Chief turns launch-night retail and Xbox Live parties into a global event.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for September 25, 2007.

items.title: The Orange Box
items.body: Valve packages Half-Life, Portal and Team Fortress 2 as a small museum of design.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for October 2007.

items.title: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare
items.body: Infinity Ward drags the military shooter into the present tense.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2007.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy
items.body: Nintendo makes gravity feel like wonder.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2007.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PS3 launches in Europe
items.body: Sony's delayed console finally reaches UK shelves with a high price and a Blu-ray pitch.
items.sourceNote: GamesIndustry.biz reports March 23, 2007.

items.title: Wii shortages continue
items.body: Nintendo's machine remains difficult to find in many markets, making scarcity part of its appeal.
items.sourceNote: Year-level retail context; verify UK stock claims with period sources.

items.title: Xbox Live defines Xbox 360
items.body: Halo 3, demos and party-like habits turn Live into a central platform feature.
items.sourceNote: Halo 3/Xbox Live context supports this reading.

items.title: DS becomes a mass-market object
items.body: Brain training, Pokemon and touch-screen software keep Nintendo handhelds visible beyond traditional players.
items.sourceNote: 2007 DS release context; verify sales claims before final copy.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Halo 3, Call of Duty 4 and Xbox Live make voice chat, matchmaking and stats part of everyday console identity.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Friends and parties
items.body: The Orange Box makes Steam feel less optional for PC players, especially around Team Fortress 2.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: Wii friend codes remain clumsy, but the console's living-room social life is enormous offline.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Forums, YouTube and emerging social media turn launch weeks into communal events.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the generation suddenly arrived all at once. Every month seemed to add another game people would still mention years later.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: Halo 3 was not just played; it was queued for, partied around and discussed through headsets.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: BioShock, Portal, Mario Galaxy and Modern Warfare made design feel newly confident in completely different directions.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: In the UK, PS3's high price, Wii scarcity and Xbox Live's strength made choosing a console feel like choosing a culture.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
